observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their cash advances.

We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances have been settled and proper accounting thereof has been made. Demand letters should be sent to concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances.

Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to consider requesting the write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation:
1.9	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Submit a reconciliation report of the intra-agency receivables and intra-agency payables variance of ₱1.806 million, otherwise, exhaust efforts to reconcile the discrepancy; and
b. Eliminate the reciprocal accounts during the preparation of the financial statements recognizing assets and liabilities in accordance with Paragraph 7 of IPSAS 1.
2.6	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit these reconciliations immediately and henceforth accurately present them in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

3.6	We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prepare and submit the CY 2024 RPCPPE immediately and henceforth ensure its timely preparation and submission in accordance with the provisions in Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2020-006.
4.11	We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular.

5.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to observe the provisions embodied in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
5.8	We also recommended and the Management agreed to prepare and properly fill up the forms and reports prescribed in the manual.

6.5	We recommended and the Human Resource Management Officer or responsible personnel agreed to submit within five (5) working days from execution/issuancethe JOCs and their supporting documents for review by the Auditor.
.

7.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2023 AAR audit observations and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor within the 60 days after receipt of the AAR.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to diligently monitor stale checks and communicate with payees for their timely encashment.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries regarding the outstanding checks.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the differences in the reported financial statement balances and implement meticulous monitoring and review measures to ensure that financial statements are henceforth recorded and presented accurately.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the required reconciliation statements and the corrected schedules/reports.
observations.reason:
Not Implemented

Reconciliation statements not provided.

Finding first covered in 2023 AAR.

Reiterated in Finding No. 2 Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to submit the disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe or upon request for timely audit of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to be vigilant in the purchase of motor vehicles and ensure that future acquisitions observe prudence and judiciousness in accordance with DBM Circular No. 2022-01.

We recommended and all officials concerned agreed to observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and R.A. 9184 and its IRR by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of checks.

We recommended and all officials concerned agreed to observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of checks.  

We also recommended and Management agreed to consider economy and efficiency in the conduct of Municipal activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2023 AAR audit observations and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor within the 60 days after receipt of the AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare and submit the RAAF on time to facilitate reconciliation and monitoring of the movement of AF on hand.

We recommended and the management agreed that payments for supplies and materials, as well as other operating expenses of the Municipality, be made directly to the suppliers in compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct BAC to be vigilant in the review of bids or quotations and ensure that purchases made are based on market prices or to the advantage of the Municipality.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the complete preparation and timely monthly submission of the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their cash advances.




We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances have been settled and proper accounting thereof has been made. It was also agreed that demand letters should be sent to concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances.

Lastly, the Municipality agreed to consider requesting the write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and the management agreed that payments for supplies and materials, as well as other operating expenses of the Municipality, be made directly to the suppliers in compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct BAC to be vigilant in the review of bids or quotations and ensure that purchases made are based on market prices or to the advantage of the Municipality.

We recommended and all officials concerned agreed to observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of checks.  


We also recommended and Management agreed to consider economy and efficiency in the conduct of Municipal activities.

We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to submit the disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe for a timely audit of accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2021 AAR audit observations and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices, and the information gathered therein to be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.  

We also recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the LGU of Sibunag shall, within 60 days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit observations and recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 

a.	Ensure that programs and projects identified in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and utilizations of the LDRRMF are in accordance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and 


b.	Ensure the complete preparation and timely monthly submission of the report on sources and utilization of DRRMF.

2.7	We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to diligently monitor stale checks and communicate with payees for their timely encashment.
2.8	We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the necessary adjusting entries regarding the outstanding checks.

3.6	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the differences in the reported financial statement balances and implement meticulous monitoring and review measures to ensure that financial statements are henceforth recorded and presented accurately.
3.7	We also recommended andthe Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the required reconciliation statements and the corrected schedules/reports.

4.9	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their cash advances.
4.10  We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from granting additional cash advances unless previous cash advances have been settled and proper accounting thereof has been made. Demand letters should be sent to concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances.
4.11    Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to consider requesting the write-off of dormant unliquidated cash advances, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
5.12	We recommendedandManagement agreed tostop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular.

6.6   We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to submit the disbursement vouchers and their corresponding supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed timeframe or upon request for timely audit of accounts.

7.8	We recommended and Management agreed to be vigilant in the purchase of motor vehicles and ensure that future acquisitions observe prudence and judiciousness in accordance with DBM Circular No. 2022-01.

8.8	We recommended and all officials concerned agreed to observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and R.A. 9184 and its IRR by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of checks.

9.8	We recommended and all officials concerned agreed to observe the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 by ensuring that proper and complete documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before the issuance of checks.
9.9	We also recommended and Management agreed to consider economy and efficiency in the conduct of Municipal activities.

10.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to observe the provisions embodied in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
10.8	We also recommended and Management agreed to prepare and properly fill up the forms and reports prescribed in the manual.

11.7	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the CY 2022 AAR audit observations and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/offices, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
11.8	We also recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, the LGU of Sibunag shall, within 60 days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit observations and recommendations.
12.6	We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare and submit the RAAF on time to facilitate reconciliation and monitoring of the movement of AF on hand.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	Reconcile the discrepancies between the account balances from financial statements and their corresponding schedules; 

b.	Prepare and submit the General Fund SCBAA, Notes to Financial Statements, and supporting schedules immediately; and 

c.	Henceforth ensure that the complete set of financial statements and supporting schedules are submitted to COA within the reglementary period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the General Services Officer or responsible personnel to submit within five working days from execution/issuance all Purchase Orders and their supporting documents for review by the Auditor.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to ensure the feasibility and timely implementation/completion of 20 percent development projects as programmed.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a.	Cause the reconciliation of the inter-agency payable account Due to LGUs of the Municipality with that of the reciprocal receivable account Due from LGUs of the Province, and effect the necessary adjusting entries on the variance noted to reflect the correct balances of  said accounts; and

b.	Conduct a periodic reconciliation of inter-agency reciprocal accounts to ensure accurate balances of payable/receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

1.	Cause the immediate issuance of Demand Letters to the following:

a.   Previous collector    and disbursing officers with unsettled accountabilities;

b.   Recipients of grants for livelihood capital for the settlement or liquidation of their accounts; and

 c.   Employeesandsuppliers/creditors with over claimed payments.

2.	Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct monitoring and analysis of the Other Receivables account; provide allowance for impairment for receivables which settlement/ collectability has been determined to be no longer certain; and to subsequently request for write-off dormant accounts, if warranted, pursuant to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:

a.	Reconcile the amount of barangay RPT shares computed manually with that which was generated by the E-TRACS;

b.	Facilitate the immediate release of the CY 2019 and 2020 RPT shares of the 13 barangays, and henceforth ensure that subsequent releases of the same are made within five days after the end of each quarter; and 

c.	Follow-up with the developer of the E-TRACS to sooner make the necessary corrections/ adjustments in the computerized system, to generate accurate computation of barangay RPT shares.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to:

a. Reconcile the amount of barangay RPT shares computed manually with that which was generated by the E-TRACS;

b. Facilitate the immediate release of the CY 2019 and 2020 RPT shares of the 13 barangays, and henceforth ensure that subsequent releases of the same are made within five days after the end of each quarter; and 

c. Follow-up with the developer of the E-TRACS to sooner make the necessary corrections/ adjustments in the computerized system, to generate accurate computation of barangay RPT shares.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the conduct and completion of the physical count of PPEs in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, in order to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts, thus, establish PPE account balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, as well as, to ensure timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Issue demand letters or notices to the AOs with long outstanding unliquidated cash advances, and if warranted, cause the withholding of their salaries pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002;
Exhaust all efforts to determine the exact address of resigned/retired accountable personnel with unsettled cash advances and send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of their cash advances; and
Request for the write-off of unliquidated cash advances which has become dormant, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Cause the immediate issuance of Demand Letters to the following:
Previous collector and disbursing officers with unsettled accountabilities;
Recipients of grants for livelihood capital for the settlement or liquidation of their accounts; and
Employees and suppliers/creditors with over claimed payments.
Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct monitoring and analysis of the Other Receivables account; provide allowance for impairment for receivables which settlement/ collectability has been determined to be no longer certain; and to subsequently request for write-off dormant accounts, if warranted, pursuant to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to formulate strategies as to work schedule and time management of concerned personnel in charge of the preparation and submission of financial reports and supporting documents, so that financial reports and its supporting documents are submitted within the required period.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to:
a. 	Hasten the implementation and completion of the remaining 2021 delayed/non-implemented development projects so that the 20 percent Development Fund is optimally utilized in the attainment of the Municipality’s socio-economic development goals; and
b. 	Extensively plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Development Plan/Annual Investment Program and ensure that the same are feasible for implementation within the timeline or as programmed.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Engineer undertake measures to comply with the provision of Items 2.1, 2.2.1 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to avoid the resulting consequences for non-compliance with the rules.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure submission of complete supporting documents for disbursements, in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations, before payment; and
Submit all lacking documents to the COA Audit Team identified in Annex D.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Direct the Inventory Committee to hasten the completion of the physical count of PPE in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, as well as, to ensure timely submission of RPCPPE to the Auditor; and

b.	Require the Property Officer to maintain complete and updated property PCs for PPEs to account for the receipt and disposition, and to reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Accountant.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to update all PPE Ledger Cards and exhaust efforts to retrieve the records pertaining to PPEs with no details, and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Cause the immediate issuance of Demand Letters to the following:

i.	Previous collector and disbursing officers with unsettled accountabilities;

ii.	Recipients of grants for livelihood capital for the settlement or liquidation of their accounts; and

iii.	Employees and suppliers/ creditors with over claimed payments.

b. Require the Municipal Accountant to regularly conduct monitoring and analysis of the Other Receivables account, and to subsequently request for write-off of dormant accounts, if warranted, pursuant to the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Issue demand letters or notices to the AOs with long outstanding unliquidated cash advances, and if warranted, cause the withholding of their salaries pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002;

b. Exhaust all efforts to determine the exact address of resigned/retired accountable personnel with unsettled cash advances and send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of their cash advances; and

c. Request for the write-off of unliquidated cash advances which has become dormant, if warranted, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Provide additional personnel to assist in the preparation and submission of financial reports and supporting documents;

b. Make representations with the AGDB to obtain monthly bank snapshots to be used as basis in the preparation of the monthly BRS; and 

c. Cause the submission of financial reports and its supporting documents within the required period by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to:

i.	Take up in the appropriate inventory account the cost of supplies, materials and relief goods procured for distribution, and in the corresponding expense account its subsequent issuance/distribution duly supported by the RIS and RSMI;

ii.	Record in the books the inventory of welfare goods for distribution in custody of the Municipality based on the inventory report prepared; and

iii.	Prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory of welfare goods procured by the Municipality.

b.	Require the Supply/Property Officer to:

i.	Coordinate with the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit an Inventory List/Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution in custody of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020 in the prescribed format in Annex H; and

ii.	Prepare and maintain Stock Cards, Requisition and Issue Slip, and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued for welfare goods.

c. Cause the submission of the duly validated/approved list of beneficiaries, proof of receipt and distribution of welfare goods to individual beneficiaries/ recipients, as well as the Inventory List/Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	Cause the inclusion of a warranty clause in succeeding Contracts/POs under Emergency Procurement of the Bayanihan Act, to ensure assurance of replacement/ recovery from goods delivered with defects, and/ or do not conform to quality standards and technical specifications of the contract; and

b.	Ensure that all Contracts/ POs contain complete information such as date of delivery and detailed item specifications pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to:

a. Exert efforts to access the GPPB Online Portal and comply with the posting requirements of all projects under the Bayanihan Act undertaken through Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases; and

b. Coordinate with the GPPB Technical Support Office so that problems in accessing the Online Portal are resolved.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute and record in the books, depreciation expenses of these PPEs, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method.
observations.reason:
Computation of depreciation expenses for Infrastructure Assets, Machinery and Equipment and Other Transportation Equipment accounts not yet undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	suspend the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remain unliquidated, despite final demand, pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004; and

b.	issue demand letters or notices to those AOs who have unliquidated cash advances granted in CYs 2012 to 2019 which were past due, and if warranted, their salaries be withheld thereafter in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.reason:
Finding first covered in 2016 AAR.

Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer immediately process the releases of the 2018 and current year’s barangay RPT shares in accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular releases shall be within five (5) days after the end of each quarter. 

We also recommended and  Management agreed that follow-ups be made to the developer of the E-TRACS, so that, necessary adjustments/corrections in the computerized system are effected for an accurate computation of barangay RPT shares.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prioritize the preparation of the RPCPPE and immediately submit the 2019 report as prescribed in the above mentioned guidelines. Timely submission of subsequent RPCPPE shall be followed in strict compliance with the rules and regulations.
observations.reason:
The last year’s report on the physical count of PPE was only finalized and submitted on June 10, 2020, thus, the updating of property records and conduct of inventory taking at year-end of CY 2020 has been delayed.

Finding first covered in 2012 AAR.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prioritize the
preparation of the RPCPPE and immediately submit the 2019 report as prescribed
in the above mentioned guidelines. Timely submission of subsequent RPCPPE
shall be followed in strict compliance with the rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with
the Municipal Engineer to secure copies of the related supporting documents such
as Project Status Report, Certificate of 100% Project Completion and Inspection and
Acceptance Report, to be used as bases to record the reclassification/transfer of
the completed project under the CIP to its appropriate PPE account. Necessary
journal entries shall then be effected in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute and
record in the books, depreciation expenses of these PPEs, using the determined
estimated useful lives and straight-line method.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. continuously provide the Provincial Accountant the data on the
Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by
the Municipality and ensure reconciliation of the balances at year-end;

b. provide the Provincial Accountant the details of the recorded balances
of fund transfers recorded in CY 2016 and prior year;

c. submit liquidation reports to the Province for fund transfers with
projects/programs already implemented/completed; and

d. verify liquidation reports already submitted to the Province and ensure
recording in the books by requesting a copy of the Journal Entry
Voucher from the Provincial Accountant.

We recommended that Management:
1. Issue Demand Letters to the following:
a) previous collector and disbursing officers with unsettled
accountabilities;
b) recipients of grants for livelihood capital for the settlement or
liquidation of their accounts; and
c) employees and suppliers/creditors with over claimed payments;
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) verify details / reconcile / adjust the various other receivables under
the Trust Fund books;
b) regularly conduct monitoring and analysis of Other Receivables; and
c) provide Allowance for Impairment to dormant Other Receivables and
subsequently request for write-off, if warranted.

We recommended that the Management:
a. suspend the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of
December 31, 2011 remain unliquidated, despite final demand, pursuant
to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004; and
b. issue demand letters or notices to those AOs who have unliquidated cash
advances granted in CYs 2012 to 2019 which were past due, and if
warranted, their salaries be withheld thereafter in accordance with
Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to ensure the submission of complete supporting documents for
disbursements pertaining to infrastructure projects implemented by
Administration, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
immediately process the releases of the 2018 and current year’s barangay RPT
shares in accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular releases shall be within
five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that follow-ups be made
to the developer of the E-TRACS, so that, necessary adjustments/corrections in the
computerized system are effected for an accurate computation of barangay RPT
shares.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to:

a. immediately remit intact the overdue accounts and those withheld in CY
2019 and subsequently, remit in full all the GSIS premiums and loans
deducted from the salaries on or before the 10th day of the month
pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291, and

b. reconcile the records pertaining to the prior years’ balance of the Due to
GSIS account and make appropriate adjustments in the books for
discrepancies, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately
submit the unsubmitted financial reports and documents and strictly comply with
the provisions of Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, Section 39 of PD
1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA, to avoid the consequences for non-compliance
with the laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of the on-
going and not implemented development projects; and
b. direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing offices
to ensure that only projects feasible and can be completed within the year
are included in the Annual Development Plan, given the limited
personnel.

We recommended and the LDRRM Council and Municipal DRRM Officer
agreed to ensure that the annual LDRRMFIP include the projects and activities
charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and the submission of the
required reports to COA, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 be strictly
complied.
We recommended and the Inventory Committee Head agreed to prioritize the preparation of the RPCPPE and immediately submit the 2018 report as prescribed in the above-mentioned guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The 2018 RPCPPE was subsequently submitted, however, the 2019 RPCPPE was not submitted as of this writing, thus, reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1 of this year’s Audit.
observations.reason: Finding first covered in 2014 Annual Audit Report (AAR)

Necessary journal entries shall then be effected in the books and appropriate ledger card and subsidiary record shall be kept and maintained thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following: 

a.	for the PPE under the accounts Other Land Improvements, Park, Plazas and Monuments and Road Networks, the Acting Supply Officer determine from the accounting records the period of the recorded transactions by going through prior years’ records and noting actual period of occurrence. For Road Networks, the Acting Supply Officer should coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office to determine the period of completion of the infrastructure projects;

b.	a physical inventory-taking of PPE under Other Land Improvements, Parks, Plaza and Monuments and Road Networks classifications be conducted based on the data obtained/results in (a) and include the same in the RPCPPE; and

c.	the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee reconcile the quantity and reported value of Land and Buildings and Other Structures between the accounting and property records to reflect accurate balances in the RPCPPE. The former shall likewise draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if warranted, to correct the value of Land in the books in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a.	the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be suspended pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and the salaries of those who have unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2012 to 2017 be withheld in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002; and
b.	in cases of retired or resigned employees, initiate issuance of demand letters to be sent to their known residence or office address.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee Head agreed to
prioritize the preparation of the RPCPPE and immediately submit the 2018
report as prescribed in the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. an Appraisal Committee be created that should prepare a
timetable to identify PPE accounts that will be given priority to
be appraised within the year and come up with appraisal values
and estimated remaining useful life of each PPE; and

b. the Municipal Accountant record in the depreciation expenses
of those appraised PPEs using the determined estimated useful
lives and straight-line method.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

a. regularly provide data to the Provincial Accountant on the
Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections
made by the Municipality and ensure reconciliation of the
balances at year-end;

b. submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for
payables recorded in 2016 and prior years; and

c. follow up recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these
are recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately
submit liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund
transfers recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province’s books.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit
copies of the supporting documents such as Project Status Report,
Certificate of Project Completion, and Inspection and Acceptance Report of
the completed projects to the Municipal Accountant as bases in the
transfer of completed projects under the CIP to its appropriate asset
accounts. Necessary journal entries shall then be effected in the books and
appropriate ledger card and subsidiary record shall be kept and maintained
thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record
the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under the
Trust Fund to the General Fund. Corresponding depreciation expenses
shall then be provided in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December
31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be
suspended pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-
004; and

b. demand letters or notices be issued to those AOs who have
unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2012 to 2018 and if
warranted, their salaries be withheld thereafter in accordance
with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to immediately process the release of the 2018 barangay
RPT shares in accordance with RA 7160. Subsequently, regular releases
shall be within five (5) days after the end of each quarter. Moreover, we
recommended and Management agreed to make representations with the
developer of the E-TRACS so that necessary adjustments/corrections in
the computerized system are made for an accurate computation of
barangay RPT shares.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 70 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the
RRSA, to avoid the consequences of non-compliance with the laws and
rules.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of
the above-mentioned development projects;

b. direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing
offices to ensure that only projects feasible and can be
completed within the year are included in the Annual
Development Plan; and

c. ensure that the implementing offices are provided with adequate
manpower and skills in the implementation of the 20% DF
projects.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Engineer agreed to ensure that contracts, purchase orders and
disbursements for infrastructure projects are supported with complete
documentation in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2012-
001.

We recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Council (MDRRMC) through the MDRRM Officer, agreed to
ensure that the PPAs in the MDRRM Plan are aligned with those of the
national council’s pre-disaster preparedness programs and activities and
that the 70% allocation of the MDRRMF are utilized only for authorized
PPAs.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to submit a
copy of the Accomplishment Report for CY 2018 to the Audit Team and
henceforth, submit the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer agreed to:

a. remit immediately the long overdue accounts and those withheld
in CY 2018;

b. reconcile the records pertaining to the prior years’ balance of the
Due to GSIS account and make appropriate adjustments in the
books for discrepancies noted during reconciliation, if
warranted; and

c. subsequently, remit in full and intact all the GSIS premiums and
loans deducted from the salaries on or before the 10th day of the
month pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that:

a.	the specific estimated useful life for each PPE be determined based on the LGU’s experience on the life of its PPE; and

b.	the depreciation expenses using the determined estimated useful lives be computed and recorded in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enjoin the LGU’s department heads to comply with the requirements of Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No. 1445 and other existing law, rules and regulations on the submission of accounts, financial records and reports, specifically:

a.	the Municipal Accountant to strictly abide with the submission of accounting records and reports within the deadline; and

b.	the Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost of completed projects and the status of on-going projects likewise submission of detailed cost of components of all infrastructure projects, among others.
We recommended and Management agreed that the paying capacity above the minimum mandated net take home pay be considered in the succeeding loan applications of employee borrowers.
observations.reason: Succeeding loans applications are not approved if below the required net take home pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:
     
a.	the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to immediately coordinate with the development partners and make schedules for the skills training of identified beneficiaries;

b.	the responsible Officers to immediately process the documents for the lot donation for the project site of the Central Trading Post; and

c.	subsequently, include in all MOA between the SA and the Municipality a provision on the prescribed timelines in the execution / implementation of BUB projects so as to provide a sense of urgency in project execution.
observations.reason: On-going implementation of projects

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:

a.	the Municipal Engineer ensure that the projects are implemented  in accordance with the approved project implementation schedule and ADM guidelines; and

b.	the projects’ documentary requirements as required in the DBM-DILG JMC 2017-3 be promptly prepared and completed before the receipt of ADM funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer agreed to:

a.	the PPAs in the MDRRM Plan be implemented in line with the strategies and objectives set for disaster preparedness, mitigation and prevention;


b.	the planned DRRM PPA be fully implemented within the target implementation period; and


c.	only pre-disaster preparedness and mitigation PPAs be funded under the 70% allocation of the MDRRMF.

observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that:

a.	appropriate adjustments shall be effected for any discrepancies noted during the reconciliation of records pertaining to the prior year balance of the Due to GSIS account;

b.	long overdue accounts shall be remitted, if warranted; and

c.	remittance of all the GSIS premiums and loans deducted from the salaries shall be henceforth be made in full and in-tact pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

a. for the PPE under the accounts Other Land Improvements, Park, Plazas and
Monuments and Road Networks, the Acting Supply Officer determine from the
accounting records the period of the recorded transactions by going through
prior years’ records and noting actual period of occurrence. For Road
Networks, the Acting Supply Officer should coordinate with the Municipal
Engineering Office to determine the period of completion of the infrastructure
projects;

b. a physical inventory-taking of PPE under Other Land Improvements, Parks,
Plaza and Monuments and Road Networks classifications be conducted based
on the data obtained/results in (a) and include the same in the RPCPPE; and

c. the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee reconcile the quantity
and reported value of Land and Buildings and Other Structures between the
accounting and property records to reflect accurate balances in the RPCPPE.
The former shall likewise draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if warranted, to
correct the value of Land in the books in accordance with the Philippine Public
Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that:

a. the specific estimated useful life for each PPE be determined based on the
LGU’s experience on the life of its PPE; and

b. the depreciation expenses using the determined estimated useful lives be
computed and recorded in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011
remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be suspended pursuant to
Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and the salaries of those who have
unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2012 to 2017 be withheld in
accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002; and

b. in cases of retired or resigned employees, initiate issuance of demand letters
to be sent to their known residence or office address.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. the Municipal Accountant regularly provide the Provincial Accountant with data
on the Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the
Municipality;

b. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit the valid current
due Province’s share in real property and other taxes which remained
unremitted by the Municipality;

c. the Municipal Accountant trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting
copies of JEVs of the Province;

d. the Municipal Accountant submit liquidation documents for fund transfers
recorded by the Province as receivable from the Municipality; and

e. the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer
evaluate the two (2) unimplemented projects, if still feasible, and expedite their
implementation.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enjoin the LGUs
department heads to comply with the requirements of Section 39 (1) (3) of PD No.
1445 and other existing law, rules and regulations on the submission of accounts, financial
records and reports, specifically:

a. the Municipal Accountant to strictly abide with the submission of accounting
records and reports within the deadline; and

b. the Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost of completed projects and
the status of on-going projects likewise submission of detailed cost of
components of all infrastructure projects, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed that the paying capacity above the
minimum mandated net take home pay be considered in the succeeding loan applications
of employee borrowers.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following: 

for the PPE under the accounts Other Land Improvements, Books, Breeding Stocks, and Road Networks, the Acting Supply Officer determine from the accounting records the period of the recorded transactions by going through prior years’ records and noting actual period of occurrence. For Road Networks, the Acting Supply Officer should coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office to determine the period of completion of the infrastructure projects;

a physical inventory-taking of PPE under Other Land Improvements, Books, Breeding Stocks, and Road Networks classifications be conducted based on the data obtained/results in (1) and include the same in the RPCPPE; and

the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee reconcile the quantity and reported value of Land between the accounting and property records to reflect accurate balances in the RPCPPE. The former shall likewise draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if warranted, to correct the value of Land in the books in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

On-going reconciliation of records.
Reconciled the Books and Breeding Stocks accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

	the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw JEV to record provision for depreciation of assets procured in CY 2016 totaling P3,453,906.25;
 
	immediately create an Appraisal Committee which should prepare a timetable to identify PPE accounts that will be given priority to be appraised within the year and come up with the appraisal values and reports on priority PPE items; and

	the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV to record provision for depreciation of those appraised PPE.
observations.reason: Still in the process of retrieving records as to the date of acquisition of PPE. Some were already provided depreciation.

The delayed submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits by the PTO hinders the regular/ prompt provision of data. regular


Other fund transfers received were not yet implemented/ utilized by the Municipality, thus, not liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:

a.	the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be suspended pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and the salaries of those who have unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2012 to 2016 be withheld in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002;

b.	in cases of deceased employees, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

c.	the Municipal Accountant stop granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason:
The management is in the process of preparing the demand to AOs with unliquidated CAs.




The management stopped granting additional cash advanced to those who still have unliquidated CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the requirements of the MOA for the four unimplemented BUB projects be immediately complied. Subsequently, compliance with the requirements provided in the MOA for all BUB projects should be within the period prescribed in the MOA and their implementation should be closely monitored.
observations.reason:
2 out of 4 unimplemented projects were implemented in 2017. The remaining 2 were unimplemented due to the following:

>Central Trading Post for Fishery Products Project has problem on lot donation.

>Seed Capital project is waiting for the skills training in coordination with development partners

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the provisions of Sections 70 and 73 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and Section 107 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA, to avoid the resulting consequences of non-compliance with the laws and rules.
observations.reason: The delayed submission of the DVs/Receipts/Payrolls and the bulk of transactions particularly in the General Fund are among the reasons of the delay in the submission of reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make representations with the Land Bank of the Philippines and follow up the confirmation of the Municipality’s registration in internet banking. Upon approval of registration, bank transactions shall be viewed and printed online and be used in the preparation of the monthly BRS to be submitted to the COA within the reglementary period. 

Meanwhile, copies of snapshots of bank statements should be secured monthly by the Municipal Accountant’s Office personnel and not by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office personnel and temporarily be made as reference in the preparation of the BRS.  Subsequently, reconciling items in the BRS shall be promptly recorded in the books.
observations.reason: Delayed bank statements/delayed submission of documents from the MTO particularly for General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay required under the GAA be enforced immediately to promote efficiency and effectiveness in delivering public service by ensuring that government employees have enough to support their daily needs.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that only those loan applications of employee borrowers with paying capacity above the minimum mandated net take home pay be approved.
observations.reason: Minimum take home pay adopted is P3,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of every AIR within 24 hours from acceptance and inspection of the procured items in consonance with Section 6.09 of COA Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular 92-386.

observations.reason: There was a problem on lot acquisition for one project. Other projects were reprogrammed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the information and details of the unexpended DRRMF in accordance with the reporting guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulation regarding the utilization of SEF.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD focal person to immediately submit the 2016 approved GPB and Accomplishment Report to the COA Audit Team. Henceforth, the said reports shall be submitted within the reglementary period as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a Task Force be created to reconcile the records pertaining to unremitted deductions and to effect immediately the remittance of long overdue accounts; and

subsequently, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit intact all the GSIS premiums and loans deducted from the salaries on or before the 10th day of the month pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.
observations.reason: Prior year balance of Due to GSIS in the amount of  P62,039.70 was not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: A complete physical count of PPEs be conducted and RPCPPE should be prepared per account; The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee reconcile the differences between their records; Draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the account balances in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that they are still in the process of reconciliation considering that most of the records pertain to prior years and pertinent documents are being retrieved to support booking up of some PPE items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that an appraisal be made for all properties without records as to the remaining estimated useful life and value. Likewise, a timeline be set for the completion of this activity within the year. Further, we recommended that the Property Custodian keep and maintain updated Property Card for each PPE account to readily provide data on the cost and actual condition of the PPE as basis in the preparation of the depreciation schedule.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant commented that the office is still in the process of retrieving and reviewing the documents supporting the acquisition/improvement/repair costs of the PPE. She explained that depreciation was provided only to some assets which have complete records, but none for those PPE which do not have records and were acquired many years back. Moreover, it was also informed that there has been difficulty in the updating of records and appraisal of all properties due to time constraints and the huge number of properties to be indexed individually as to their dates of acquisition, cost and actual condition, thus, their depreciated value cannot be determined immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: Effect immediately the remittance to the Province of Guimaras of its shares in the real property tax collections made by the Municipality; Trace the details of the fund transfers and submit liquidations to the Province of Guimaras; Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant disclosed that the continued non-reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts was due to the failure of the Provincial Accountant to provide the municipality with data on the Province’s receivable from the Municipality and the latter’s payable to the former. A meeting was held among the Provincial Accountant and the Municipal Accountants to settle the differences in the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: The GAD focal person submit the Annual GPB to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval thru the Provincial Government and furnish the COA Audit Team the approved GPB. The GAD Focal Person submit a copy of the Accomplishment Report for CY 2015 to the Audit Team and henceforth, submit the same within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:


        a. for the PPE under the accounts Other Land  Improvements,  Books,
           Breeding Stocks, and Road Networks, the  Acting  Supply  Officer
           determine from the accounting records the period of the recorded
           transactions by going through prior years’  records  and  noting
           actual period of  occurrence.  For  Road  Networks,  the  Acting
           Supply Officer should coordinate with the Municipal  Engineering
           Office  to  determine  the   period   of   completion   of   the
           infrastructure projects;


        b.  a  physical  inventory-taking   of   PPE   under   Other   Land
           Improvements,  Books,  Breeding  Stocks,   and   Road   Networks
           classifications be conducted based on the data  obtained/results
           in (a) from accounting records  and  include  the  same  in  the
           RPCPPE; and


        c. the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory  Committee  reconcile
           the quantity and reported value of Land between  the  accounting
           and property records to reflect accurate balances in the RPCPPE.
           The former shall likewise draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), if
           warranted, to  correct  the  value  of  Land  in  the  books  in
           accordance  with  the  Philippine   Public   Sector   Accounting
           Standards (PPSAS).

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:


        a. the Municipal Accountant  to  immediately  draw  JEV  to  record
           provision  for  depreciation  of  assets  procured  in  CY  2016
           totaling P3,453,906.25;


        b. immediately create an Appraisal Committee which should prepare a
           timetable to identify PPE accounts that will be  given  priority
           to be appraised within the year and come up with  the  appraisal
           values and reports on priority PPE items; and


        c. the Municipal Accountant to draw JEV  to  record  provision  for
           depreciation of those appraised PPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:

        a. regularly provide the Provincial Accountant  with  data  on  the
           Province’s share in the real property and other tax  collections
           made by the Municipality;


        b. remit the valid current due Province’s share  in  real  property
           and other taxes which remained unremitted by the Municipality;

        c. trace the details of the fund transfers by requesting copies  of
           JEVs of the Province;


        d. submit liquidation documents for fund transfers recorded by  the
           Province as Due from LGUs; and


        e. conduct periodic reconciliation with the Provincial Accountant.

We recommended and Management agreed that:


        a. the salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31,
           2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand,  be  suspended
           pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular  No.  2012-004  and  the
           salaries of those who have unliquidated cash advances granted in
           CY 2012 to 2016 be withheld in accordance with  Section  5.9  of
           COA Circular 97-002;


        b.  in  cases  of  deceased  employees,   initiate   write-off   of
           unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA  Circular  No.
           2016-005 dated December 19,  2016; and


        c. the Municipal Accountant stop granting additional cash  advances
           to those who have unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the requirements  of  the
   MOA for the four unimplemented  BUB  projects  be  immediately  complied.
   Subsequently, compliance with the requirements provided in  the  MOA  for
   all BUB projects should be within the period prescribed in  the  MOA  and
   their implementation should be closely monitored.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to  comply  with
   the provisions of Sections 70 and 73 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, and
   Section 107 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA, to avoid  the
   resulting consequences of non-compliance with the laws and rules.

We  recommended  and  the  Municipal  Accountant  agreed  to   make
   representations with the Land Bank of the Philippines and follow  up  the
   confirmation of the Municipality’s registration in internet banking. Upon
   approval of registration, bank transactions shall be viewed  and  printed
   online and be used in the preparation of the monthly BRS to be  submitted
   to the COA within the reglementary period.


        Meanwhile, copies of snapshots of bank statements should  be  secured
   monthly by the Municipal Accountant’s Office personnel  and  not  by  the
   Municipal  Treasurer’s  Office  personnel  and  temporarily  be  made  as
   reference in the preparation of the BRS.  Subsequently, reconciling items
   in the BRS shall be promptly recorded in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that  the  prescribed  minimum
   employee’s monthly net take home pay required under the GAA  be  enforced
   immediately to promote efficiency and effectiveness in delivering  public
   service by ensuring that government  employees  have  enough  to  support
   their daily needs.


        We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed  that  only
   those loan applications of employee borrowers with paying capacity  above
   the minimum mandated net take home pay be approved.

We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to furnish the  Office
   of the Auditor a copy of every AIR within 24 hours  from  acceptance  and
   inspection of the procured items in consonance with Section 6.09  of  COA
   Circular No. 95-006 and COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended and the Municipal Development  Council  through  the
   Municipal Planning and Development Officer agreed to ensure that:


        a. the  utilization  of  the  20%  Development  Fund  be  optimally
           utilized;


        b. projects identified in the plan are feasible and  with  complete
           documentation;

        c. projects are properly scheduled and can  be  implemented  within
           the year with the proposed budget allocation; and


        d. projects  which  are  discontinued  or  abandoned  such  as  the
           Improvement of School Building are declared savings and utilized
           in accordance with the guidelines and procedures in LBC No.  112
           dated  June 10, 2016.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to  observe  the
   disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the  information  and
   details  of  the  unexpended  DRRMF  in  accordance  with  the  reporting
   guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly  adhere  with  the
   provisions of the existing  laws,  rules  and  regulation  regarding  the
   utilization of SEF.

We recommended and Management  agreed  to  require  the  GAD  focal
   person to immediately submit the 2016  approved  GPB  and  Accomplishment
   Report to the COA Audit Team.  Henceforth,  the  said  reports  shall  be
   submitted within the reglementary period as prescribed  in  COA  Circular
   No. 2014-001.

We recommended and Management agreed that:


        a. a Task Force be created to reconcile the records  pertaining  to
           unremitted deductions and to effect immediately  the  remittance
           of long overdue accounts; and


        b.  subsequently,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the   Municipal
           Treasurer remit intact all the GSIS premiums and loans  deducted
           from the salaries on  or  before  the  10th  day  of  the  month
           pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to identify the completed projects recorded under the CIP accounts and prepare a Journal Entry Voucher duly supported with schedules to transfer the CIP accounts to their appropriate agency asset or infrastructure accounts.
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the provisions of applicable Executive Order as may be implemented by pertinent Budget Circular in the grant of PEI.

We recommended that: The salaries of employees whose cash advances as of December 31, 2011 remained unliquidated, despite final demand, be suspended pursuant to Section 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and the salaries of those who have unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2012 to 2015 be withheld in accordance with Section 5.9 of COA Circular 97-002; In cases of deceased employees, initiate write-off of unliquidated cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997; The Municipal Accountant avoid granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that several of the long overdue accounts were subject for reconciliation and adjustments. However, for other unliquidated CAs granted after CY 2011, they were not able to issue a letter of demand or its equivalent to the concerned AOs, both active and non-active from government service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that strict monitoring be done on the submission of accounts/reports and sanctions be imposed to those at fault and cause the delay of the submission considering that this has been the prevalent practice since CY2009.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant explained that the delayed submission of Trial Balances in the General Fund is due mainly to the lack of technically competent personnel thus forcing the Accountant to almost singlehandedly do the encoding and journalizing of transactions in the GF. In addition, the Municipal Treasurer’s Office was also usually delayed in the submission of Official Receipts and disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the BRS monthly for all funds so as to reflect the book reconciling items in the period to which it pertains.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, the delay in the receipt of the bank statements from Land Bank of the Philippines contributed to the delay in the preparation of BRS. It was also informed that the Municipality has already enrolled in the online / internet banking facility; however, they are still waiting for the bank’s confirmation of their registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enforce the prescribed minimum employee’s monthly net take home pay required under the GAA to promote efficiency and effectiveness in delivering public service by ensuring that government employees have enough to support their daily needs. We also recommended and Management agreed that the paying capacity of each employee borrower above the minimum mandated net take home pay be considered prior to the approval of loan applications.
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor did not approve loans of employees with below minimum monthly net take home pay starting in CY 2016 but there were still employees who have pending loans acquired in previous years which were not yet fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that proper planning and scheduling be made in the formulation of annual development plan giving consideration for unanticipated events that may occur and will affect the implementation of the planned projects so that all projects identified under the 20% DF be fully implemented/completed during the year.
observations.reason: Delayed implementation of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to strictly adhere to the provisions of the existing laws, rules and regulation regarding the utilization of SEF.
observations.reason: Several financial assistance still charged from the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: A Task Force be created to reconcile the records pertaining to unremitted deductions and to effect immediately the remittance of long overdue accounts. Subsequently, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit intact all the GSIS premiums and loans deducted from the salaries on or before the 10th day of the month pursuant to Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of GSIS accounts on-going.
